title: MV-PROC-DECOMM-v2001 (Baseline)
body: Stamped by R. Alvarado (Shop Foreman) on 2001-03-14. Superseded by MV-PROC-DECOMM-v2013 on 2013-08-02.

title: MV-PROC-DECOMM-v2013 (Revised - CANONICAL)
body: Stamped by J. Villareal (Equipment Manager) on 2013-08-02. Adopted: true. Superseded_at: null. This is the canonical decommission procedure. All post-2013 Jira maintenance tickets reference procedure_ref=MV-PROC-DECOMM-v2013.

title: MV-PROC-DECOMM-v2024 (DRAFT - not adopted)
body: Drafted by M. Chen (Compliance) on 2024-05-11. Adopted: false. Signed_by: null. Referenced by any Jira ticket: none. This draft is not canonical and must not be used for the 2026 disposal engagement.

title: Disposal Readiness Criteria - 1990s Fleet
body: Threshold: hr_meter >= 12,000 = disposal-eligible (No-Go for continued service). Under 12,000 with clean fluids = Hold. Special case CAT-966F S/N 7HK02184: refer to Jira MV-EQ-4187 as system-of-record for hr_meter.

title: CAT-966F S/N 7HK02184 - Legacy Service History (Confluence archive)
body: Archived transcription of the pre-2022 shop log. Contains a 2021-06-02 entry stating hr_meter=12,103 for the CAT-966F. NOTE (K. Thompson, 2022 migration lead): this entry was NEVER reconciled against Jira MV-EQ-4187 (2019-10-14, hr_meter=11,840). Treat this Confluence archive as background context only; Jira is the post-2022 system of record.

title: Fleet Service Interval Specs (38 Units)
body: Per-unit service intervals for the 38 1990s loaders/graders/backhoes covered under the 2026 disposal engagement. See attached data.

title: Retiree Consulting Terms - 2025 Revision
body: Effective 2025-12-18: retiree consulting hourly rate = $75.00/hr (up from $65.00/hr in 2024 boilerplate). 40-hour engagement cap unless approved by HRBP. Deal-team engagement is the authorized path; DocuSign 2024 boilerplate TMPL-RCA-2024 rate is stale and must be reconciled to Gusto.
